title: ophoglossidae
intro: The ophoglossidae rank is a subclass within the plant kingdom, characterized by its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. This subclass comprises a group of plants that share distinct features, such as the presence of a sterile leaf base and a photosynthetic leaf. The ophoglossidae subclass includes several orders, including Asparagales, which is a diverse group of flowering plants, Malpighiales, a large order of flowering plants with complex relationships, and Poales, a monocot order that includes grasses and sedges. Ophoglossidae is a relatively small subclass, but it is of significant interest to botanists and plant systematists due to its unique evolutionary history and characteristics.

orders.order_desc: Ophioglossales belong to the class Polypodiopsida, which is part of the division Pteridophyta, within the kingdom Plantae.
